input.background: An inmate on death row in the state of Illinois has a WAIS of 51.
input.question: What percentage of people have a score below this level?

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: Again, the four-step procedure is used: 1. Statel $X \sim N(100,15)$. We want to determine $\operatorname{Pr}(X<51)$ 2. Standardize: $z=\frac{51-100}{15}=-3.27$ 3. Sketch: not shown 4. Table B: $\operatorname{Pr}(Z \leq-3.27)=0.0005$ (about $0.05 \%$ )

WAIS_score.description: The WAIS (Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scale) score of the inmate.

percentage_below_score.description: The percentage of people who have a WAIS score below 51.
